sum(f-) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)

prose:
Method provenance – Formaldehyde Fukui reactivity (electrophilic Fukui function, f+)
Property: atomic Fukui function f+ values; headline: most electrophilic site value = 0.7083913 (atom 2, carbon).
Level of theory: DFT, tier ‘standard’ → B3LYP/def2-TZVP with density fitting, as implemented in PySCF 2.x (backend used by chemkit).
Geometry provenance: fixed geometry taken from the user-supplied file /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/formaldehyde/formaldehyde.xyz; no re-optimization was performed (single-point N, N ± 1 charge-state calculations).
Solvent / environment: gas phase (no implicit solvation model).
Charge and multiplicity: neutral singlet (charge 0, multiplicity 1) for the N-electron reference; cation run at charge +1, doublet; anion run at charge –1, doublet.
Software + version: chemkit fukui-reactivity skill calling PySCF DFT engine (B3LYP functional, def2-TZVP basis, RI integral approximation on by default).
Convergence & caveats: SCF cycles for all three charge states converged (integrity.status = "ok"; integrity.trustworthy = true). Warnings returned by the engine (verbatim):
• "sum(f+) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)"
• "sum(f-) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)"
• "common header present"
The sums exactly equal 1.0000, so the charge-conservation checks passed; the messages are informational.
Reproducibility: command executed –
/…/cli.py fukui --method dft --tier standard --charge 0 --multiplicity 1 /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/formaldehyde/formaldehyde.xyz --out …/live.json
A live stdout/stderr log was written during the run to the same job directory (path provided at run start); the result JSON is available at …/live.json. The Fukui plot file is saved at /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/formaldehyde/argo_o3/20260703-193619_formaldehyde_fukui_reactivity/agent_call_01_plot.png.
